content: Where did the secret of Samson's strength lie?
explanation: Judges 16:17 — Samson's strength came from his Nazirite vow to God, symbolized in his uncut hair.

content: In the book of Judges, whom did the LORD raise up to deliver the people?
explanation: Judges 2:16 — The LORD raised up judges to deliver the people from their oppressors. Cycle: sin > oppression > cry out > deliverance.

content: What is the recurring cycle in the book of Judges?
explanation: Judges 2:11-19 — This cycle is repeated many times, showing human nature and God's patience.
options: Sin > punishment > crying out > deliverance > peace > sin again

content: Who is the only female judge in the Bible?
explanation: Judges 4:4 — Deborah was both a prophetess and a judge who judged Israel.

content: Who killed the commander Sisera with a tent peg?
explanation: Judges 4:21 — Jael drove a tent peg through Sisera's temple while he slept. Victory comes from unexpected places.

content: When the angel called Gideon a 'mighty man of valor,' what was he doing?
explanation: Judges 6:11-12 — Gideon was hiding in fear, but God saw the potential in him.
options: Beating wheat in the winepress, hiding from the Midianites

content: What did Gideon use to test God?
explanation: Judges 6:37-40 — Gideon tested God twice with a fleece of wool. God patiently bore his weakness.
options: A fleece of wool (with dew, then dry)

content: From 32,000, to how many men did the LORD reduce Gideon's army?
explanation: Judges 7:7 — Just 300 men, so that God would receive the glory. Victory does not come by numbers but by God's power.

content: What did Jephthah vow to the LORD before the battle?
explanation: Judges 11:30-31 — Jephthah's hasty vow led to heartbreaking consequences when his own daughter came out to greet him.
options: To offer as a burnt offering whatever first came out of his door to meet him

content: What was Samson set apart to be from the womb?
explanation: Judges 13:5 — Samson was a Nazirite from the womb, set apart to begin saving Israel.

content: What animal did Samson tear apart with his bare hands by the Spirit of the LORD?
explanation: Judges 14:6 — The Spirit of the LORD rushed upon Samson, and he tore the lion as one tears a young goat.

content: What weapon did Samson use to strike down 1,000 Philistines?
explanation: Judges 15:15 — Samson struck down 1,000 men with the fresh jawbone of a donkey.

content: How did Samson die?
explanation: Judges 16:30 — Samson prayed one last time, was given strength, and pushed down the two pillars. He killed more in his death than in his life.
options: Pushed down the Philistine temple, dying with the enemies

content: After Joshua died, which tribe did the LORD say should go up first against the Canaanites?
explanation: Judges 1:1-2 — 'After the death of Joshua, the people of Israel inquired of the LORD... The LORD said, Judah shall go up.'

content: Ehud, the left-handed judge, killed which king of Moab?
explanation: Judges 3:21-22 — 'Ehud reached with his left hand, took the sword... and thrust it into his belly. And the hilt also went in after the blade.'

content: Who sang the victory song in Judges 5?
explanation: Judges 5:1 — 'Then sang Deborah and Barak the son of Abinoam on that day.'

content: Abimelech was killed by what object thrown by a woman from the tower?
explanation: Judges 9:53 — 'And a certain woman threw an upper millstone on Abimelech's head and crushed his skull.'

content: The Ephraimites were detected and killed at the fords of the Jordan when they could not pronounce what word?
explanation: Judges 12:6 — 'They said to him, Then say Shibboleth, and he said, Sibboleth, for he could not pronounce it right. Then they seized him and slaughtered him at the fords of the Jordan.'

content: What is the famous summary of the time of the judges in Judges 17:6?
explanation: Judges 17:6 — 'In those days there was no king in Israel. Everyone did what was right in his own eyes.'
options: There was no king; everyone did what was right in his own eyes
God dwelt in the midst of the people

content: The brutal account in Judges 19 involves the concubine of which man being violated at Gibeah?
explanation: Judges 19:1,25-30 — A Levite's concubine was abused to death by men of Gibeah, leading to civil war with Benjamin.
